trajectory.description: Starting from scratch. I need to build a complete HMER pipeline: CNN encoder (DenseNet121 pretrained) + Transformer decoder. First iteration goal: validate end-to-end pipeline with a short 3-epoch training run, then submit eval to measure iteration time and get a baseline exprate. I will use AMP for speed, dynamic batch padding, and label smoothing.

trajectory.command: python -m py_compile model.py predict.py train.py && python -c "import torch; from model import HMERModel; m=HMERModel(10,max_len=32); x=torch.randn(2,1,160,200); out=m.beam_search(x,1,2,beam_width=3,max_len=32); print('beam_search ok', out.shape)"

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 1 completed a 3-epoch training run achieving 1.58% exprate with greedy decoding. The pipeline works end-to-end. Epochs 2-3 took ~10 min each after a slow first epoch (~85 min, likely cold file cache). With ~4.2h remaining, a 10-12 epoch training run is feasible (~3.5h). Analysis: Before committing to a long training run, I should (1) validate an improved predict.py with beam search on the existing checkpoint to quantify inference-time gains, and (2) update training code with LR scheduling, label smoothing, and proper metrics logging for the next run. Decision: This iteration updates model.py (adds beam_search), predict.py (uses beam search), and train.py (adds OneCycleLR, label smoothing, jsonl logging). I will run a quick local syntax/unit test, then submit eval with the existing 3-epoch checkpoint to measure beam-search exprate without burning training time.

Observation: Iteration 2 validated beam search on the 3-epoch checkpoint, raising exprate from 1.58% to 2.25%. The pipeline is solid but severely undertrained. Analysis: With ~4.16h left, I can commit to a longer training run. Key improvements needed: (1) data augmentation (rotation, scale, translate) for handwritten variability, (2) label smoothing + OneCycleLR for better convergence, (3) LayerNorm after encoder projection and dropout in decoder for stability, (4) larger batch size (48) and persistent_workers to improve throughput, (5) proper jsonl logging so curves populate. Decision: Write updated model.py, train.py, and predict.py, then launch a 15-epoch training run. I'll use batch_size=48 to reduce steps-per-epoch and keep total runtime under the budget.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 3 crashed after ~10 min with a torch.inductor RuntimeError ('shape invalid for input of size 15') caused by dynamic spatial dimensions when compiling the DenseNet encoder. Analysis: torch.compile does not handle the per-batch variable image widths safely in our HMER pipeline. The workspace also had truncated model.py (beam_search incomplete) and train.py. Decision: Rewrite complete model.py and train.py in eager mode (no torch.compile), adding tgt padding masks, gradient clipping, and proper jsonl logging. Run a 3-epoch smoke test to confirm stability, then submit eval to re-baseline before committing to a long run.

Observation: Iteration 4 completed a 3-epoch eager-mode training run achieving 3.92% exprate with beam search (new best). The command took ~75 min for 3 epochs, but prior epochs may have been slowed by cold data caches. The model is still severely undertrained (loss ~1.8, random-guess loss would be ~4.9). Analysis: With ~2.65h remaining, the highest-ROI action is to resume training from the existing best checkpoint and train significantly longer. The pipeline is stable, so I should minimize architectural changes to avoid breaking the working checkpoint. Decision: Write a complete train.py that resumes from checkpoints/best.pt, trains until epoch 15 (12 more epochs), and saves the best checkpoint each epoch. I will add label smoothing, gradient clipping, and proper jsonl logging. I will also inspect the checkpoint first to verify resume metadata.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 5 trained for 12 additional epochs (to epoch 15) but validation exprate only reached 2.23% and training loss flat-lined at ~1.72 from epoch 3 onwards. Analysis: The loss plateau strongly indicates the OneCycleLR schedule decayed the learning rate to near-zero by epoch 15, halting progress. The model is severely underfitting (loss still far from convergence). Decision: Resume from the latest checkpoint but reinitialize the optimizer and scheduler to CosineAnnealingLR with warmup, add weight decay and label smoothing, and train for 40 more epochs. Simultaneously submit eval with the current best checkpoint to verify its test score.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 6 trained for 40 additional epochs (to epoch 55) and achieved 14.93% test exprate with beam width 5, a massive improvement. Training loss is still slowly decreasing (1.03 at epoch 55) while validation loss has plateaued (~1.28), suggesting mild overfitting but room for improvement with a learning-rate reset. Remaining time is ~71 minutes, enough for roughly 30 more epochs at ~100s per epoch. Analysis: The highest-ROI action is to resume from the latest checkpoint, reinitialize optimizer/scheduler to jump-start learning again, and train for another 30 epochs. Simultaneously, increasing inference beam width from 5 to 10 can squeeze extra accuracy at no training cost. Decision: Update train.py to resume from checkpoints/latest.pt with a fresh AdamW + cosine annealing schedule (lr=1e-4, 30 epochs, 3-epoch warmup), and update predict.py default beam_width to 10. Run training now; submit eval with best.pt in the next iteration.
